    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cross the Threshold](/Feats.aspx?ID=2713) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #166: Despair on Danger Island](/Sources.aspx?ID=85) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Ghost Eater](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=92)

**Prerequisites**
[Ghost Eater Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2710)

</column>

---

You can cast [_ethereal jaunt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=105) as an occult innate spell once per day. You don't need to Sustain the Spell; instead, it lasts for 10 minutes or until you choose to return to your material form as a free action.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Jellyfish Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2729) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=152" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Feats.aspx?ID=4077)**

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 75, [Fists of the Ruby Phoenix Hardcover](/Sources.aspx?ID=193) pg. 212

**Requirements**
You are unarmored.

</column>

---

You relax your posture and loosen your joints, allowing yourself to move with incredible fluidity. You can make stinging lash attacks that deal 1d8 slashing damage. These attacks are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group, and have the [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

 While in Jellyfish Stance, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves and on checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) and [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Jellyfish Stance
requirement_markdown: You are unarmored.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Golden League Xun Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2734) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 76

**Archetype** [Golden League Xun](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=93)

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

You are a xun for the Golden League, aiding the organization. You gain expert proficiency in two of the following skills (or in two other skills of your choice in which you're trained, if you were already an expert in the listed skills): [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15). In addition, if you use Underworld Lore to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) or [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), when you get a success, you get a critical success instead.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the Golden League Xun archetype.

<title level="2">Golden League Xun Dedication leads to...</title>
[Beneath Notice](/Feats.aspx?ID=2735), [Menacing Prowess](/Feats.aspx?ID=2736), [Rope Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=2737), [Silent Sting](/Feats.aspx?ID=2738)
name: Golden League Xun Dedication

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Rope Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=2737) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 76

**Archetype** [Golden League Xun](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=93)

**Prerequisites**
[Golden League Xun Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2734); Expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

Ropes are your tool of choice, and you deploy them in versatile ways. When [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) a rope, you move 5 more feet on a success and 10 more feet on a critical success, to a maximum of your Speed. This additional movement stacks with the effects of [Quick Climb](/Feats.aspx?ID=821).

 In addition, you can use a rope to hamper your foes. When holding a rope, you can attempt an Athletics check to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) a creature up to 10 feet away instead of only adjacent creatures. If you critically fail such a check while holding a rope, you can drop the rope to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure.
name: Rope Mastery

>
[Silent Sting](/Feats.aspx?ID=2738) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #167: Ready? Fight!](/Sources.aspx?ID=86) pg. 76

**Archetype** [Golden League Xun](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=93)

**Prerequisites**
[Golden League Xun Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2734); Expert in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Trigger**
You make a Strike with a weapon of light bulk.

</column>

---

You quickly hide your weapon to make it difficult to pin the attack on you. You attempt to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) to hide the triggering weapon. Your Stealth check is compared to the Perception DC of all creatures within 30 feet that can see you. You automatically succeed against creatures farther than 30 feet. The target of your Strike gains a +4 circumstance bonus to their Perception DC for this check. If your Stealth check is a success, you hide your weapon so well that the creature can't clearly determine where the attack originated.
name: Silent Sting

[Sixth Pillar Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2739) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />

**Prerequisites**
Dexterity 14; Expert in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1); ability to cast spells

</column>

---

You use your physical discipline to channel your magic. If your proficiency rank in unarmed attacks is at least expert, your proficiency rank in spell attacks increases to expert. Likewise, if your proficiency rank in spell attacks is at least expert, your proficiency rank in unarmed attacks improves to expert. You become an expert in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), or in another trained skill if you were already expert in Athletics.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you've gained two other feats from the Sixth Pillar archetype.

<title level="2">Sixth Pillar Dedication leads to...</title>
[Maneuvering Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2741), [Six Pillars Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2740), [Sixth Pillar Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=2742), [Sixth Pillar Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=4078), [Touch Focus](/Feats.aspx?ID=2743), [Touch Focus](/Feats.aspx?ID=4079)

---

You can redirect a spell's power through you to deliver it within striking distance. If your next action is to Cast a Spell with 1 or more targets and a range farther than touch, you can change the range to touch. If the altered spell requires a spell attack roll, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack. This only allows you to touch a single target with the spell, even if the spell originally affected more than 1 target. After you Cast the Spell, you can [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38), [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40), or Strike the target with an unarmed attack.

    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[All the Time in the World](/Feats.aspx?ID=2744) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Investigator" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=318" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 75

**Frequency**
once per hour

</column>

---

Your firsthand experience of the subjective nature of time and space has given you new observational insights. Your mind processes information so quickly that time appears to stop for all creatures but you. You can take up to 9 actions in 3 sets of up to 3 actions each. During this time, you can use purely mental and observational abilities such as [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), and [Devise a Stratagem](/Actions.aspx?ID=546), and you can use an action to move a mental projection of yourself to a location within a 60- foot radius to observe objects more closely and from different angles, though you can't affect your surroundings in any way (for instance, you could move your mental projection more closely to a distant door to read a minute inscription on its handle, but you could not open it to observe the other side). When the duration elapses, time resumes its normal flow.

<title level="2">Capstone Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 75

Player characters can select one of the following new class feats for their 20th-level class feat upon entering the _Glass Lighthouse_ or by meditating on the cushion in area H4.

**Related Feats**: [Dance of Intercession](/Feats.aspx?ID=2745), [Demon's Hair](/Feats.aspx?ID=2746), [Everdistant Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=2747), [Extradimensional Stash](/Feats.aspx?ID=2748), [Head of the Night Parade](/Feats.aspx?ID=2749), [Heart of the Kaiju](/Feats.aspx?ID=2750), [Plum Deluge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2751), [Ruby Resurrection](/Feats.aspx?ID=2752), [Scapegoat Parallel Self](/Feats.aspx?ID=2753), [Sever Space](/Feats.aspx?ID=2754), [Time Dilation Cascade](/Feats.aspx?ID=2755), [Vitality-Manipulating Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2756), [Vivacious Afterimage](/Feats.aspx?ID=2757), [Whirlwind Toss](/Feats.aspx?ID=2758), [Worldsphere Gravity](/Feats.aspx?ID=2759)
name: All the Time in the World

You Stride in a dance up to half your Speed, attempting a DC 35 Performance check. You can perform this dance up to three times per day. The second time you do so in the same day, use the degree of success one worse than your actual roll on the Performance check. The third time in a day, use the degree of success two lower than your actual roll. **Critical Success** You perform the movements of the Dance of Intercession so gracefully that you evoke a glimmer of the memory of the Celestial Dragon's awesome power. You cast the 3-action version of either 9th-level [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) or [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) at any point during your Stride. This does not use any spell slots.
**Success** As critical success, but the spell is 7th level instead of 9th as you stumble through the movements.
**Failure** As critical success, but the spell is 5th level instead of 9th as you stumble through the movements.
**Critical Failure** You fail to remember the steps of the dance. You gain no additional effect beyond Striding half your Speed, and you can't attempt the Dance of Intercession again until your next daily preparations. Your hair constantly wriggles and writhes like that of your rival, Syu Tak-nwa. Your damage die of your hair unarmed Strike increases by one step and it gains the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) and [versatile slashing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) traits. Your familiar assumes partial control over your hair; as long as your familiar is alive and within 30 feet, you are permanently [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32), and you can use the extra action only to make a hair Strike. You can deliver [hexes](/Traits.aspx?ID=324) through your hair. When you successfully cast a non-cantrip hex that requires 2 or more actions to cast and that doesn't require a spell attack roll, if your target is within your reach, as part of the spellcasting activity you can make a hair Strike against them before applying any effects of the hex. If this Strike misses, the hex has no effect.

**Special** If your hair already has the reach trait, it instead gains the [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194) trait.

The spatial barrier of Syndara's Island has taught you that distance can foil enemies better than any armor. You warp space, causing each square within your aura that the attack or creature would move through to count as triple the distance. May contain spoilers from Fists of the Ruby Phoenix **Source** [Pathfinder #168: King of the Mountain](/Sources.aspx?ID=87) pg. 76 **Prerequisites** [Monstrosity Shape](/Feats.aspx?ID=349) --- Witnessing the might of the kaiju King Mogaru, Alru, and Toraloa up close awoke a new ferocity within you. When you transform using Monstrosity Shape, you can take on a kaiju form. When you take this feat, choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage; this is the damage your breath weapon deals. You can't change this later. The battle form is Gargantuan size with the following specific abilities; the damage values already include the extra die from being a 9th-level spell.
  • **Kaiju** Speed 40 feet; physical resistance 5; **Melee** jaws ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+20 piercing; **Melee** claws ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+20 slashing;

You have learned to saturate an area with poison in imitation of the techniques of your rival, Blue Viper. As part of this ability, you Interact to draw three vials of the same contact poison and throw them in the air, where they shatter and spread a poisonous deluge affecting a 20-foot burst within 60 feet. All creatures in the burst are automatically exposed to the poison, immediately bypassing any onset time, and must attempt a saving throw against that poison. The three vials must be identical, even if the poison can come in multiple types or different levels.

You move so quickly that you create a short-lived afterimage, looks exactly like you, in front of you or behind you. You Stride. At any point along your movement, you leave an afterimage of yourself behind. Alternatively, you can stop moving and send your afterimage forward instead, up to the remainder of your Speed. A creature that spends actions to engage with your afterimage can attempt to disbelieve it with a Will save against your class DC. Once an enemy has disbelieved any of your afterimages, they are temporarily immune to all of your Vivacious Afterimages for 10 minutes. As long as a creature has not disbelieved the afterimage, the afterimage counts as a creature for flanking. The afterimage lasts until the beginning of your next turn or until you use Vivacious Afterimage again, whichever comes first.

Your canny understanding of natural and primal creatures helps you predict and dodge their attacks. The triggering attack roll targets your [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) DC instead of your AC. You can launch a mass of quills at opponents. You spray quills in a 30-foot cone, dealing 5d8 piercing damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. At 11th level and every even level thereafter, the piercing damage increases by 1d8 and the persistent bleed damage increases by 1.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Raise a Tome](/Feats.aspx?ID=2848) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 42

**Requirements**
You're holding a book.

</column>

---

You raise up the book you're holding and flip it open to defend yourself and expedite your studies. The book remains raised until the start of your next turn. While you have a book raised, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and a +1 circumstance bonus to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) to identify creatures using a skill related to the subject of the book ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) if you're using your spellbook). This bonus is in addition to any item bonus the book normally gives to the check.

 If you have [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839), you can use the tome for that feat (Hardness 3, HP 12, BT 6) as though it were a shield. Whenever you use an ability that allows you to Raise a Shield, such as [Emergency Targe](/Feats.aspx?ID=2855), you can Raise a Tome instead, changing any requirements that normally require a shield to apply to your book.

<title level="2">Raise a Tome leads to...</title>
[Shielded Tome](/Feats.aspx?ID=2862)
name: Raise a Tome
requirement_markdown: You're holding a book.

    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Expansive Spellstrike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2849) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 43

**Prerequisites**
[Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755)

</column>

---

You can blast the energy of a spell out around the target of your attack. When you [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755) with a spell that has an area of a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385), [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), or [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), the spell creates an area instead of affecting only the target. The target must be in the area. A burst is centered on a corner of the target’s square; if the target is Large or larger, the burst use the square corner closest to the center of the target and choose the corner if more than one is eligible. A cone or line emits from you and must include the target; if you’re not adjacent to the target (using a [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) weapon or [starlit span](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=4), for example), choose any square adjacent to the target as the source. The spell affects all creatures in the area as normal, but the Strike still targets only one creature.
name: Expansive Spellstrike

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spirit Sheath](/Feats.aspx?ID=2852) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Extradimensional" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=67" />
<trait label="Magus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=389" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 44

</column>

---

You've magically constructed an extradimensional sheath for your weapon that you access through your clothing, typically through a pocket, sleeve, or seam. This sheath is hard to notice, granting a +2 circumstance bonus to your [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Conceal an Item](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) within it. A spirit sheath can hold only one weapon at a time, and the weapon must be 1 Bulk or less.

During a [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755), you can Interact to draw the weapon before you Cast the Spell. You can draw or return a weapon in a spirit sheath as an Interact action, as can anyone else holding or wearing the clothing that contains the sheath. A [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell or similar effect can be used against the spirit sheath to expel the weapon. The [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check is made against your spell DC and uses the sheath's counteract level of 1. The weapon is also expelled if you become [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38).
name: Spirit Sheath

You've studied the staff extensively to learn the hidden capabilities of your chosen weapon. When you critically succeed at an attack roll using a staff, you apply the [critical specialization effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=235) of the [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) group. While you're in Arcane Cascade stance, your staff gains the [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait, with the damage from the deadly die being the same damage type as the extra damage from Arcane Cascade.

 In addition, you can place [property runes](/Equipment.aspx?Category=23&Subcategory=27) into a magic staff you prepare, even though a magic staff can't typically hold property runes. You must first inscribe the property runes into your [spellbook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=53), using the same process as [transferring a rune](/Rules.aspx?ID=737). The rune takes the form of a specialized diagram in your spellbook, occupying one page. You can't inscribe runes that can't be placed on a non-magical staff, nor can you inscribe the [_shifting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=302) rune. When you prepare a staff, you can impart any number of property runes from your spellbook into the staff, up to the limit imposed by the staff's [_weapon potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281) rune.

When you prepare a [staff](/Equipment.aspx?Category=32), you can hold it up to a weapon to merge the two items. You can switch the fused item's form from the weapon to the staff or vice versa as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. When the item is in staff form, you can Cast the Spells from the staff and benefit from any other abilities the staff grants. The staff and the weapon share their fundamental runes, using whichever _[weapon potency](/Equipment.aspx?ID=281)_ and whichever _[striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280)_ rune is higher level. They don't share any other runes or specific abilities.

 You can Cast Spells from the staff as part of a Spellstrike even when the staff is in weapon form. Otherwise, you can't cast the staff's spells while it's in weapon form.

 The fusion lasts until your next daily preparations, though you can spend 10 minutes to separate the two items and meld the staff into a different weapon. The melding works for you alone. A different wielder can use the staff in its current form but can't transform it, get the benefit of the shared runes, or use its spells for a Spellstrike if it's in weapon form.
name: Fused Staff

 If you use [Expansive Spellstrike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2849) to Spellstrike with an area spell, apply the area as described in Expansive Spellstrike, choosing one of the creatures you Strike to determine the area and whether the spell fails due to a critically failed Strike. You can use this activity with only melee Strikes, even if you have the [starlit span](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=4) hybrid study or a similar ability.

Your physical form can combine with that of your eidolon, granting benefits but limiting your capabilities. You [Manifest your Eidolon](/Actions.aspx?ID=757), but instead of summoning it into an adjacent open space, you become it. While Manifested in this way, you use its statistics, and you can't act except to direct it to use Manifest an Eidolon to unmanifest it. Since you can't act, you can't Cast Spells, activate or benefit from magic items that normally benefit you and not your eidolon, perform actions that have the [tandem](/Traits.aspx?ID=395) trait, or use other abilities that require you, and not the eidolon, to act. Your can't be separately targeted while you are melded into it. When you reach 0 HP, your eidolon unmanifests, leaving your body behind, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) and [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=11). You can Meld Into Eidolon while your eidolon is already manifested as long as it's adjacent to you.

Since you work together to move, your eidolon's [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions while you're mounted gain the [tandem](/Traits.aspx?ID=395) trait.

<title level="3">Riding Sapient Creatures</title> Riding along on a sapient creature that isn't a minion requires a lot of coordination and timing. Both the riding creature and the mount regain only 2 actions at the start of their turns each round, as both the mount and the riding creature interfere with one another's actions. If you ride your eidolon, you reduce your total actions to 2 and continue to share actions normally—you don't reduce the number of actions twice.
